observation: I can see the sign-up page for Deriv. There are social login options (Google, Facebook, Apple) at the top, followed by an email input field and a red 'Sign up' button. There's also language selection overlays open on both sides of the screen. As an elderly user with limited tech experience, I should use the email signup option rather than social logins. The email field has placeholder text 'name@email.com' and there's a prominent red 'Sign up' button below it. I need to enter an email address first.
reasoning: As a 70-year-old user, I prefer straightforward email signup over social media logins which I'm less familiar with. The email input field is clearly visible and large enough for me to use. I should enter a test email address to proceed with the registration flow.
ux_issues: Language selection overlays are covering parts of the screen which could be confusing
Multiple signup options might be overwhelming for elderly users
